chunk_summary: This section outlines standardized techniques for MUAC and oedema measurement in children, emphasizing accuracy, common errors, and clinical interpretation to assess malnutrition severity.
learning_objectives: Understand the step-by-step procedure for accurate MUAC measurement in children aged 6–59 months.
Identify and correct common errors in MUAC and oedema assessment techniques.
Learn the clinical significance of bilateral pitting oedema and its grading in malnutrition diagnosis.
key_facts: MUAC <115 mm is a diagnostic cutoff for Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM) in children aged 6–59 months.
The midpoint of the left upper arm is identified between the acromion and olecranon for MUAC measurement.
High-frequency errors include incorrect landmarks, tape twisting, and improper tape tension (too loose or too tight).
Bilateral pitting oedema is graded as mild (feet only), moderate (up to legs or hands), or severe (generalized including face).
Persistent pitting after pressing both thumbs on the dorsum of the feet indicates oedema.
reference_quote: "Always document the age band because standard cutoffs (e.g., <115 mm for SAM) apply to children 6–59 months." (Source: Facility Based Care of Severe Acute Malnutrition — Participant Manual, MoHFW, Government of India)
